qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended CPU to GPU ratio for 28-core Volta nodes on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: You should scale the number of CPUs requested, keeping the ratio of CPUs to GPUs at 3.5 or less on 28 core nodes.

qa_pairs.question: How many CPU cores should I request for a job using 4 GPUs on a 28-core Volta node?
qa_pairs.answer: For example, if you want to run a job using 4 GPUs, you should request at most 14 CPU cores.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum number of CPU cores recommended for a single GPU job on a 28-core Volta node?
qa_pairs.answer: For a job with 1 GPU, you should request at most 3 CPU cores.

qa_pairs.question: Are users allowed to run test jobs on Volta nodes that do not adhere to the recommended CPU to GPU ratio?
qa_pairs.answer: Users are allowed to run a few short test jobs (shorter than 1 hour) that break this rule to see how your code performs.

qa_pairs.question: What CPU to GPU ratio should be used for the two newest 40-core Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: The two newest Volta nodes have 40 cores so the number of cores requested per GPU should be adjusted upwards accordingly, i.e. you can use 5 CPU cores per GPU.

qa_pairs.question: What is a key benefit of using the newest 40-core Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: They also have NVLINK, which can provide huge benefits for situations where memory bandwidth between GPUs is the bottleneck.

qa_pairs.question: How can I specifically request one of the NVLINK-enabled Volta nodes with 40 cores for my job?
qa_pairs.answer: If you want to use one of these NVLINK nodes, you should request it directly by adding the `--constraint=cascade,v100` parameter to the job submission script.

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide an example of a job script for running a single V100 GPU job on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer:
Single-GPU example:
{{File
  |name=gpu_single_GPU_job.sh
  |lang="sh"
  |contents=
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --account=def-someuser
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:v100:1
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=3
#SBATCH --mem=12G
#SBATCH --time=1-00:00
module load arch/avx512 StdEnv/2018.3
nvidia-smi
}}

qa_pairs.question: Can you provide an example of a job script for running a full Volta node job (8 GPUs) on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer:
Full-node example:
{{File
  |name=gpu_single_node_job.sh
  |lang="sh"
  |contents=
#!/bin/bash
#SBATCH --account=def-someuser
#SBATCH --nodes=1
#SBATCH --gres=gpu:v100:8
#SBATCH --exclusive
#SBATCH --cpus-per-task=28
#SBATCH --mem=150G
#SBATCH --time=1-00:00
module load StdEnv/2023
nvidia-smi
}}

qa_pairs.question: Where should I store temporary files for I/O intensive jobs on Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: The Volta nodes have a fast local disk, which should be used for jobs if the amount of I/O performed by your job is significant. Inside the job, the location of the temporary directory on fast local disk is specified by the environment variable $SLURM_TMPDIR.

qa_pairs.question: Are files stored in the $SLURM_TMPDIR on Volta nodes automatically cleaned up after a job finishes?
qa_pairs.answer: All the files in $SLURM_TMPDIR will be removed once the job ends, so you do not have to clean up that directory yourself.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to create Python virtual environments within the temporary job space ($SLURM_TMPDIR) on Volta nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: You can even create Python virtual environments in this temporary space for greater efficiency.

qa_pairs.question: How do I specify Turing GPU cards when submitting a job on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: When requesting Turing GPU nodes, you should specify: `--gres=gpu:t4:2`.

qa_pairs.question: How do I specify Ampere GPU cards when submitting a job on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: When requesting Ampere GPU nodes, you should specify: `--gres=gpu:a100:2` or `--gres=gpu:a5000:2`.

qa_pairs.question: When will the Graham cluster's operational capacity be reduced?
qa_pairs.answer: Starting January 13, 2025, the Graham cluster will operate at approximately 25% capacity until the new system Nibi comes online.

qa_pairs.question: What will be Graham's operational capacity starting January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Starting January 13, 2025, the Graham cluster will operate at approximately 25% capacity until the new system Nibi comes online.
